name: weights

members.name: AlexNetWeights
members.extended:
Currently ships a single ImageNet-1k checkpoint
(`IMAGENET1K_V1`) — the canonical single-stream OWT weights
distributed by the reference-framework model zoo, re-hosted under
`huggingface.co/lucid-dl/alexnet` with the published
`acc@1=56.522 / acc@5=79.066` validation metrics.

members.name: DenseNet121Weights
members.extended: 121-layer DenseNet (growth-rate 32, 8.0 M params, top-1 74.43%).
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import densenet_121_cls
>>> model = densenet_121_cls(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Huang, Liu, van der Maaten, Weinberger, *"Densely
Connected Convolutional Networks"*, CVPR 2017 ([arXiv:1608.06993](https://arxiv.org/abs/1608.06993)).

attributes.name: IMAGENET1K_V1
attributes.description:
ImageNet-1k V1 checkpoint (top-1 74.434% / top-5 91.972%),
sourced from `torchvision/DenseNet121_Weights.IMAGENET1K_V1`.

notes:

members.name: VGG11BNWeights
members.extended: VGG-A with BatchNorm (132.9 M params, 7.6 GFLOPs, top-1 70.37%).
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import vgg_11_bn_cls
>>> model = vgg_11_bn_cls(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Simonyan, Zisserman, *"Very Deep Convolutional Networks
for Large-Scale Image Recognition"*, ICLR 2015 ([arXiv:1409.1556](https://arxiv.org/abs/1409.1556)).
BatchNorm added in the torchvision reimplementation (not in the
original paper).

members.name: ResNet18Weights
members.extended:
Ships a single ImageNet-1k checkpoint (`IMAGENET1K_V1`) — the
canonical V1 weights distributed by the reference-framework model
zoo, re-hosted under `huggingface.co/lucid-dl/resnet-18` with the
official `acc@1=69.758 / acc@5=89.078` validation metrics.
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import resnet_18_cls
>>> model = resnet_18_cls(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: He, Zhang, Ren, Sun, *"Deep Residual Learning for Image
Recognition"*, CVPR 2016 ([arXiv:1512.03385](https://arxiv.org/abs/1512.03385)).

attributes.name: IMAGENET1K_V1
attributes.description:
ImageNet-1k V1 checkpoint (top-1 69.758% / top-5 89.078%),
sourced from `torchvision/ResNet18_Weights.IMAGENET1K_V1`.

notes:

2016 ([arXiv:1605.07146](https://arxiv.org/abs/1605.07146)).

members.name: ResNeXt50_32x4dWeights
members.extended: ResNeXt-50 (32 groups × 4d width, 25.0 M params, top-1 81.20%).
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import resnext_50_32x4d_cls
>>> model = resnext_50_32x4d_cls(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Xie, Girshick, Dollár, Tu, He, *"Aggregated Residual
Transformations for Deep Neural Networks"*, CVPR 2017
([arXiv:1611.05431](https://arxiv.org/abs/1611.05431)).

attributes.name: IMAGENET1K_V2
attributes.description:
ImageNet-1k V2 checkpoint (top-1 81.198% / top-5 95.340%),
sourced from `torchvision/ResNeXt50_32X4D_Weights.IMAGENET1K_V2`.

members.name: ResNeXt101_32x4dWeights
members.extended:
Sourced from the timm Gluon `gluon_in1k` checkpoint — note the
bicubic interpolation and 0.875 crop_pct (256 resize → 224 crop)
rather than the bilinear / 232-resize preset of the torchvision V2
variants.
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import resnext_101_32x4d_cls
>>> model = resnext_101_32x4d_cls(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Xie, Girshick, Dollár, Tu, He, *"Aggregated Residual
Transformations for Deep Neural Networks"*, CVPR 2017
([arXiv:1611.05431](https://arxiv.org/abs/1611.05431)).

attributes.name: GLUON_IN1K
attributes.description:
timm Gluon ImageNet-1k checkpoint (top-1 80.342% / top-5
94.926%), sourced from `timm/resnext101_32x4d.gluon_in1k`.

members.name: ResNeXt101_32x8dWeights
members.extended: ResNeXt-101 (32 groups × 8d width, 88.8 M params, top-1 82.83%).
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import resnext_101_32x8d_cls
>>> model = resnext_101_32x8d_cls(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Xie, Girshick, Dollár, Tu, He, *"Aggregated Residual
Transformations for Deep Neural Networks"*, CVPR 2017
([arXiv:1611.05431](https://arxiv.org/abs/1611.05431)).

attributes.name: IMAGENET1K_V2
attributes.description:
ImageNet-1k V2 checkpoint (top-1 82.834% / top-5 96.228%),
sourced from `torchvision/ResNeXt101_32X8D_Weights.IMAGENET1K_V2`.

members.name: InceptionV3Weights
members.extended: Szegedy et al. CVPR 2016 (23.8 M params, top-1 77.29% on ImageNet-1k).
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import inception_v3_cls
>>> model = inception_v3_cls(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Szegedy, Vanhoucke, Ioffe, Shlens, Wojna, *"Rethinking the
Inception Architecture for Computer Vision"*, CVPR 2016
([arXiv:1512.00567](https://arxiv.org/abs/1512.00567)).

attributes.name: IMAGENET1K_V1
attributes.description:
ImageNet-1k V1 checkpoint (top-1 77.294% / top-5 93.450%),
sourced from `torchvision/Inception_V3_Weights.IMAGENET1K_V1`.

members.name: InceptionNeXtTinyWeights
members.extended:
Ships a single ImageNet-1k checkpoint (`SAIL_IN1K`) converted
from timm's `inception_next_tiny.sail_in1k` weights and re-hosted
under `huggingface.co/lucid-dl/inception-next-tiny`.

members.name: InceptionNeXtSmallWeights
members.extended:
Ships a single ImageNet-1k checkpoint (`SAIL_IN1K`) converted
from timm's `inception_next_small.sail_in1k` weights and re-hosted
under `huggingface.co/lucid-dl/inception-next-small`.

members.name: InceptionNeXtBaseWeights
members.extended:
Ships a single ImageNet-1k checkpoint (`SAIL_IN1K`) converted
from timm's `inception_next_base.sail_in1k` weights and re-hosted
under `huggingface.co/lucid-dl/inception-next-base`.  Uses a
tighter `crop_pct=0.95` eval pipeline (236 resize / 224 crop).

members.name: InceptionResNetV2Weights
members.extended: Szegedy et al. AAAI 2017 (55.8 M params, top-1 80.46%).
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import inception_resnet_v2_cls
>>> model = inception_resnet_v2_cls(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Szegedy, Ioffe, Vanhoucke, Alemi, *"Inception-v4,
Inception-ResNet and the Impact of Residual Connections on
Learning"*, AAAI 2017 ([arXiv:1602.07261](https://arxiv.org/abs/1602.07261)).

attributes.name: TF_IN1K
attributes.description:
TensorFlow-Slim ImageNet-1k checkpoint (top-1 80.46% / top-5
95.31%), sourced from `timm/inception_resnet_v2.tf_in1k`.

members.name: XceptionWeights
members.extended:
Ships a single ImageNet-1k checkpoint (`TF_IN1K`) converted
from timm's `legacy_xception.tf_in1k`, re-hosted under
`huggingface.co/lucid-dl/xception`.

members.name: MobileNetV2Weights
members.extended:
Ships a single ImageNet-1k checkpoint (`IMAGENET1K_V1`) —
the V1 weights distributed by the reference-framework model zoo,
re-hosted under `huggingface.co/lucid-dl/mobilenet-v2` with the
official `acc@1=71.878 / acc@5=90.286` validation metrics.

members.name: MobileNetV3LargeWeights
members.extended: Howard et al. ICCV 2019 Large (5.5 M params, top-1 74.04%).
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import mobilenet_v3_large_cls
>>> model = mobilenet_v3_large_cls(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Howard et al., *"Searching for MobileNetV3"*, ICCV 2019
([arXiv:1905.02244](https://arxiv.org/abs/1905.02244)).

attributes.description:
ImageNet-1k V1 checkpoint (top-1 74.042% / top-5 91.340%),

`torchvision/MobileNet_V3_Large_Weights.IMAGENET1K_V1`.

members.name: SEResNet18Weights
members.extended: SE-ResNet-18 (Hu et al. CVPR 2018; 11.8 M params, top-1 70.6%).
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import se_resnet_18_cls
>>> model = se_resnet_18_cls(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Hu, Shen, Sun, *"Squeeze-and-Excitation Networks"*, CVPR
2018 ([arXiv:1709.01507](https://arxiv.org/abs/1709.01507)).

members.name: SEResNet34Weights
members.extended: SE-ResNet-34 (Hu et al. CVPR 2018; 22.0 M params, top-1 73.31%).

>>> model = se_resnet_34_cls(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Hu, Shen, Sun, *"Squeeze-and-Excitation Networks"*, CVPR

members.name: SEResNet50Weights
members.extended:
Sourced from timm's `seresnet50.ra2_in1k` RandAugment recipe — a
cleanly SE-augmented ResNet whose state-dict naming matches Lucid
one-for-one (identity key map).
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import se_resnet_50_cls
>>> model = se_resnet_50_cls(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Hu, Shen, Sun, *"Squeeze-and-Excitation Networks"*, CVPR
2018 ([arXiv:1709.01507](https://arxiv.org/abs/1709.01507)).

attributes.name: RA2_IN1K
attributes.description:
timm RandAugment-v2 ImageNet-1k checkpoint (top-1 78.498%),
sourced from `timm/seresnet50.ra2_in1k`.

members.extended: SE-ResNet-101 (Hu et al. CVPR 2018; 49.3 M params, top-1 78.32%).

notes:
Reference: Hu, Shen, Sun, *"Squeeze-and-Excitation Networks"*, CVPR

>>> model = efficientnet_b3_cls(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Tan, Le, *"EfficientNet: Rethinking Model Scaling for
Convolutional Neural Networks"*, ICML 2019 ([arXiv:1905.11946](https://arxiv.org/abs/1905.11946)).

attributes.name: IMAGENET1K_V1

ImageNet-1k V1 checkpoint (top-1 82.008% / top-5 96.054%),
sourced from `torchvision/EfficientNet_B3_Weights.IMAGENET1K_V1`.

members.name: SKResNet18Weights
members.extended:
Single ImageNet-1k checkpoint (`RA_IN1K`) converted from
`timm/skresnet18.ra_in1k` — Wightman's RandAugment recipe, hosted
under `huggingface.co/lucid-dl/sk-resnet-18` with the official
`acc@1 = 73.020 / acc@5 = 91.172` validation metrics.

members.name: SKResNet34Weights
members.extended:
Single ImageNet-1k checkpoint (`RA_IN1K`) converted from
`timm/skresnet34.ra_in1k` — Wightman's RandAugment recipe, hosted
under `huggingface.co/lucid-dl/sk-resnet-34` with the official
`acc@1 = 76.956 / acc@5 = 93.320` validation metrics.

members.name: CSPDarknet53Weights

notes:
Reference: Wang, Liao, Wu, Chen, Hsieh, Yeh, *"CSPNet: A New Backbone
that can Enhance Learning Capability of CNN"*, CVPRW 2020
([arXiv:1911.11929](https://arxiv.org/abs/1911.11929)).

members.name: CrossViT9Weights

examples:
>>> from lucid.models import crossvit_9_cls
>>> model = crossvit_9_cls(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Chen, Fan, Panda, *"CrossViT: Cross-Attention Multi-Scale
Vision Transformer for Image Classification"*, ICCV 2021
([arXiv:2103.14899](https://arxiv.org/abs/2103.14899)).

members.name: CrossViT15Weights
members.extended: 15-block CrossViT variant (Chen et al. ICCV 2021; 27.5 M params, top-1 81.5%).

notes:
Reference: Chen, Fan, Panda, *"CrossViT: Cross-Attention Multi-Scale
Vision Transformer for Image Classification"*, ICCV 2021
([arXiv:2103.14899](https://arxiv.org/abs/2103.14899)).

examples:

notes:
Reference: Chen, Fan, Panda, *"CrossViT: Cross-Attention Multi-Scale
Vision Transformer for Image Classification"*, ICCV 2021
([arXiv:2103.14899](https://arxiv.org/abs/2103.14899)).

notes:
Reference: Chen, Fan, Panda, *"CrossViT: Cross-Attention Multi-Scale
Vision Transformer for Image Classification"*, ICCV 2021
([arXiv:2103.14899](https://arxiv.org/abs/2103.14899)).

Introducing Convolutions to Vision Transformers"*, ICCV 2021
([arXiv:2103.15808](https://arxiv.org/abs/2103.15808)).

Introducing Convolutions to Vision Transformers"*, ICCV 2021
([arXiv:2103.15808](https://arxiv.org/abs/2103.15808)).

members.name: CvTW24Weights
members.extended: wide CvT-w24 IN-22k→IN-1k 384 finetune (Wu et al.; 277 M params, top-1 87.7%).
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import cvt_w24_cls
>>> model = cvt_w24_cls(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Wu, Xiao, Codella, Liu, Dai, Yuan, Zhang, *"CvT:
Introducing Convolutions to Vision Transformers"*, ICCV 2021
([arXiv:2103.15808](https://arxiv.org/abs/2103.15808)).

attributes.name: IN22K_FT_IN1K_384
attributes.description:
ImageNet-22k pretrain + ImageNet-1k 384x384 finetune (top-1
87.7%), sourced from `transformers/microsoft/cvt-w24-384-22k`.

members.name: ConvNeXtTinyWeights
members.extended:
Currently ships `IMAGENET1K_V1` — torchvision's ImageNet-1k
checkpoint (acc@1 = 82.520 / acc@5 = 96.146) re-hosted under
`huggingface.co/lucid-dl/convnext-tiny`.

members.name: ConvNeXtSmallWeights
members.extended:
Currently ships `IMAGENET1K_V1` — torchvision's ImageNet-1k
checkpoint (acc@1 = 83.616 / acc@5 = 96.650).

members.name: ConvNeXtBaseWeights
members.extended:
Currently ships `IMAGENET1K_V1` — torchvision's ImageNet-1k
checkpoint (acc@1 = 84.062 / acc@5 = 96.870).

members.name: ConvNeXtLargeWeights
members.extended:
Currently ships `IMAGENET1K_V1` — torchvision's ImageNet-1k
checkpoint (acc@1 = 84.414 / acc@5 = 96.976).

members.name: ConvNeXtXLargeWeights
members.extended:
Currently ships `FB_IN22K_FT_IN1K` — Facebook AI Research's
ImageNet-22k pretraining → ImageNet-1k finetune checkpoint
(paper Table 11, acc@1 ≈ 87.0%), sourced via timm's
`convnext_xlarge.fb_in22k_ft_in1k` and re-hosted under
`huggingface.co/lucid-dl/convnext-xlarge`.  torchvision does not
publish a 1k-class ConvNeXt-XLarge head.

members.name: PVTv2B0Weights
members.extended:
Single ImageNet-1k checkpoint converted from timm's
`pvt_v2_b0.in1k` (Wang et al., 2022; ~3.7M params, 70.5% top-1).

members.name: PVTv2B1Weights
members.extended:
Single ImageNet-1k checkpoint converted from timm's
`pvt_v2_b1.in1k` (Wang et al., 2022; ~14.0M params, 78.7% top-1).

members.name: PVTv2B2Weights
members.extended:
Single ImageNet-1k checkpoint converted from timm's
`pvt_v2_b2.in1k` (Wang et al., 2022; ~25.4M params, 82.0% top-1).

members.name: PVTv2B3Weights
members.extended:
Single ImageNet-1k checkpoint converted from timm's
`pvt_v2_b3.in1k` (Wang et al., 2022; ~45.2M params, 83.1% top-1).

members.name: PVTv2B4Weights
members.extended:
Single ImageNet-1k checkpoint converted from timm's
`pvt_v2_b4.in1k` (Wang et al., 2022; ~62.6M params, 83.6% top-1).

members.name: PVTv2B5Weights
members.extended:
Single ImageNet-1k checkpoint converted from timm's
`pvt_v2_b5.in1k` (Wang et al., 2022; ~82.0M params, 83.8% top-1).

members.name: GoogLeNetWeights
members.extended:
Single ImageNet-1k checkpoint converted from torchvision's
`GoogLeNet_Weights.IMAGENET1K_V1` (Szegedy et al., 2015; ~13.0M
params with auxiliary heads, 69.778% top-1).

members.name: DETRResNet50Weights
members.extended:
Single COCO 2017 checkpoint converted from Facebook DETR's
`detr_resnet50` reference checkpoint (Carion et al., 2020; ~41.5M
params, 42.0 box mAP).

members.name: DETRResNet101Weights
members.extended:
Single COCO 2017 checkpoint converted from Facebook DETR's
`detr_resnet101` reference checkpoint (Carion et al., 2020; ~60.5M
params, 43.5 box mAP).

members.name: FCNResNet50Weights
FCNResNet101Weights
FasterRCNNResNet50FPNWeights
members.extended:
Single COCO checkpoint converted from the reference
`FasterRCNN_ResNet50_FPN_Weights.COCO_V1` (91 classes, box AP 37.0).

members.name: MaskRCNNResNet50FPNWeights
members.extended:
Single COCO checkpoint converted from the reference
`MaskRCNN_ResNet50_FPN_Weights.COCO_V1` (91 classes, box AP 37.9 /
mask AP 34.6).

members.name: MaskFormerResNet50Weights
members.extended:
Single ADE20k semantic checkpoint converted from
`facebook/maskformer-resnet50-ade` (Cheng et al., 2021; ~41.3M
params, 44.5 mIoU on the ADE20k validation set).

members.name: MaskFormerResNet101Weights
members.extended:
Single ADE20k semantic checkpoint converted from
`facebook/maskformer-resnet101-ade` (Cheng et al., 2021; ~60.3M
params, 45.5 mIoU on the ADE20k validation set).

members.name: Mask2FormerSwinTinyWeights
Mask2FormerSwinSmallWeights
Mask2FormerSwinBaseWeights
Mask2FormerSwinLargeWeights
members.extended:
Single ADE20k semantic checkpoint converted from
`facebook/mask2former-swin-large-ade-semantic` (Cheng et al., 2022;
~215M params, 56.1 mIoU on the ADE20k validation set — the headline
paper result).

members.name: MobileNetV1Weights
EfficientFormerL1Weights
members.extended:
Single ImageNet-1k checkpoint converted from timm's
`efficientformer_l1.snap_dist_in1k` (Li et al., 2022; ~12.3M params,
79.2% top-1).

members.name: EfficientFormerL3Weights
members.extended:
Single ImageNet-1k checkpoint converted from timm's
`efficientformer_l3.snap_dist_in1k` (Li et al., 2022; ~31.4M params,
82.4% top-1).

members.name: EfficientFormerL7Weights
members.extended:
Single ImageNet-1k checkpoint converted from timm's
`efficientformer_l7.snap_dist_in1k` (Li et al., 2022; ~82.2M params,
83.3% top-1).

members.name: ViTBase16Weights
ViTBase32Weights
ViTLarge16Weights
members.extended:
Single ImageNet-1k checkpoint converted from torchvision's
`ViT_L_16_Weights.IMAGENET1K_V1` (Dosovitskiy et al., 2021;
~304.3M params, 79.662% top-1).  Uses a 242-pixel resize before the
224 center crop (vs. 256 for the other variants).

members.name: ViTLarge32Weights
ResNeSt50Weights
members.extended:
Single ImageNet-1k checkpoint converted from timm's
`resnest50d.in1k` (Zhang et al., 2022; ~27.5M params, 81.1%
top-1).  Evaluated at 224 crop / 256 resize / bilinear.

members.name: ResNeSt101Weights
members.extended:
Single ImageNet-1k checkpoint converted from timm's
`resnest101e.in1k` (Zhang et al., 2022; ~48.3M params, 82.8%
top-1).  Evaluated at 256 crop / 293 resize / bilinear.

members.name: ResNeSt200Weights
members.extended:
Single ImageNet-1k checkpoint converted from timm's
`resnest200e.in1k` (Zhang et al., 2022; ~70.2M params, 83.9%
top-1).  Evaluated at 320 crop / 352 resize / bicubic.

members.name: ResNeSt269Weights
members.extended:
Single ImageNet-1k checkpoint converted from timm's
`resnest269e.in1k` (Zhang et al., 2022; ~110.9M params, 84.5%
top-1) — the deepest paper-cited variant.  Evaluated at 416 crop /
448 resize / bicubic.

members.name: SwinTinyWeights
members.extended:
Ships a single ImageNet-1k checkpoint (`IMAGENET1K_V1`)
converted from the reference-framework `Swin_T_Weights` and
re-hosted under `huggingface.co/lucid-dl/swin-tiny`.

members.name: SwinSmallWeights
members.extended:
Ships a single ImageNet-1k checkpoint (`IMAGENET1K_V1`)
converted from the reference-framework `Swin_S_Weights` and
re-hosted under `huggingface.co/lucid-dl/swin-small`.

members.name: SwinBaseWeights
members.extended:
Ships a single ImageNet-1k checkpoint (`IMAGENET1K_V1`)
converted from the reference-framework `Swin_B_Weights` and
re-hosted under `huggingface.co/lucid-dl/swin-base`.

members.name: SwinLargeWeights
members.extended:
Ships the original Microsoft ImageNet-22k → ImageNet-1k finetune
(`MS_IN22K_FT_IN1K`), re-hosted via timm
(`swin_large_patch4_window7_224.ms_in22k_ft_in1k`) under
`huggingface.co/lucid-dl/swin-large`.  The paper (Liu et al.,
2021, Table 2) reports 86.3% top-1 for this checkpoint at 224×224.

members.name: MaxViTTinyWeights
members.extended:
Single ImageNet-1k checkpoint converted from timm's
`maxvit_tiny_tf_224.in1k` (Tu et al., 2022; ~30.9M params,
83.62% top-1).

members.name: MaxViTSmallWeights
members.extended:
Single ImageNet-1k checkpoint converted from timm's
`maxvit_small_tf_224.in1k` (Tu et al., 2022; ~68.9M params,
84.45% top-1).

members.name: MaxViTBaseWeights
members.extended:
Single ImageNet-1k checkpoint converted from timm's
`maxvit_base_tf_224.in1k` (Tu et al., 2022; ~119.5M params,
84.95% top-1).

members.name: MaxViTLargeWeights
members.extended:
Single ImageNet-1k checkpoint converted from timm's
`maxvit_large_tf_224.in1k` (Tu et al., 2022; ~211.8M params,
85.17% top-1).

members.name: BERTTinyWeights
members.extended: Turc et al. 2019 `L=2, H=128, A=2` distilled encoder (4.4 M params).
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import bert_tiny
>>> model = bert_tiny(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Turc, Chang, Lee, Toutanova, *"Well-Read Students Learn
Better"*, 2019 ([arXiv:1908.08962](https://arxiv.org/abs/1908.08962)).

attributes.name: WIKIPEDIA_BOOKSCORPUS
attributes.description:
Wikipedia + BookCorpus pre-training checkpoint sourced from
`transformers/google/bert_uncased_L-2_H-128_A-2`.

members.name: BERTMiniWeights
members.extended: Turc et al. 2019 `L=4, H=256, A=4` distilled encoder (11.2 M params).
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import bert_mini
>>> model = bert_mini(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Turc, Chang, Lee, Toutanova, *"Well-Read Students Learn
Better"*, 2019 ([arXiv:1908.08962](https://arxiv.org/abs/1908.08962)).

attributes.name: WIKIPEDIA_BOOKSCORPUS
attributes.description:
Wikipedia + BookCorpus pre-training checkpoint sourced from
`transformers/google/bert_uncased_L-4_H-256_A-4`.

members.name: BERTSmallWeights
members.extended: Turc et al. 2019 `L=4, H=512, A=8` distilled encoder (28.8 M params).
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import bert_small
>>> model = bert_small(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Turc, Chang, Lee, Toutanova, *"Well-Read Students Learn
Better"*, 2019 ([arXiv:1908.08962](https://arxiv.org/abs/1908.08962)).

attributes.name: WIKIPEDIA_BOOKSCORPUS
attributes.description:
Wikipedia + BookCorpus pre-training checkpoint sourced from
`transformers/google/bert_uncased_L-4_H-512_A-8`.

members.name: BERTMediumWeights
members.extended: Turc et al. 2019 `L=8, H=512, A=8` distilled encoder (41.4 M params).
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import bert_medium
>>> model = bert_medium(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Turc, Chang, Lee, Toutanova, *"Well-Read Students Learn
Better"*, 2019 ([arXiv:1908.08962](https://arxiv.org/abs/1908.08962)).

attributes.name: WIKIPEDIA_BOOKSCORPUS
attributes.description:
Wikipedia + BookCorpus pre-training checkpoint sourced from
`transformers/google/bert_uncased_L-8_H-512_A-8`.

members.name: BERTBaseWeights
members.extended: Devlin et al. 2018 `L=12, H=768, A=12` uncased encoder (109.5 M params).
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import bert_base
>>> model = bert_base(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Devlin, Chang, Lee, Toutanova, *"BERT: Pre-training of
Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding"*, NAACL
2019 ([arXiv:1810.04805](https://arxiv.org/abs/1810.04805)).

attributes.name: WIKIPEDIA_BOOKSCORPUS
attributes.description:
Wikipedia + BookCorpus pre-training checkpoint sourced from
`transformers/google-bert/bert-base-uncased`.

members.name: BERTLargeWeights
members.extended: Devlin et al. 2018 `L=24, H=1024, A=16` uncased encoder (335.1 M params).
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import bert_large
>>> model = bert_large(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Devlin, Chang, Lee, Toutanova, *"BERT: Pre-training of
Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding"*, NAACL
2019 ([arXiv:1810.04805](https://arxiv.org/abs/1810.04805)).

attributes.name: WIKIPEDIA_BOOKSCORPUS
attributes.description:
Wikipedia + BookCorpus pre-training checkpoint sourced from
`transformers/google-bert/bert-large-uncased`.

members.name: BERTBaseMLMWeights
members.extended: BERT-Base encoder + tied MLM head over the 30 522 WordPiece vocab.
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import bert_base_mlm
>>> model = bert_base_mlm(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Devlin, Chang, Lee, Toutanova, *"BERT: Pre-training of
Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding"*, NAACL
2019 ([arXiv:1810.04805](https://arxiv.org/abs/1810.04805)).

attributes.name: WIKIPEDIA_BOOKSCORPUS
attributes.description:
Wikipedia + BookCorpus pre-training checkpoint (encoder + MLM
head) sourced from `transformers/google-bert/bert-base-uncased`.

members.name: BERTLargeMLMWeights
members.extended: BERT-Large encoder + tied MLM head over the 30 522 WordPiece vocab.
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import bert_large_mlm
>>> model = bert_large_mlm(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Devlin, Chang, Lee, Toutanova, *"BERT: Pre-training of
Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding"*, NAACL
2019 ([arXiv:1810.04805](https://arxiv.org/abs/1810.04805)).

attributes.name: WIKIPEDIA_BOOKSCORPUS
attributes.description:
Wikipedia + BookCorpus pre-training checkpoint (encoder + MLM
head) sourced from `transformers/google-bert/bert-large-uncased`.

members.name: BERTBaseQAWeights
members.extended: BERT-Base encoder + 2-way span head; F1 88.1 / EM 80.9.
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import bert_base_qa
>>> model = bert_base_qa(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Devlin, Chang, Lee, Toutanova, *"BERT: Pre-training of
Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding"*, NAACL
2019 ([arXiv:1810.04805](https://arxiv.org/abs/1810.04805)).  Fine-tuned on SQuAD v1.1.

attributes.name: SQUAD_V1
attributes.description:
SQuAD v1.1 fine-tuned checkpoint (F1 88.1 / EM 80.9), sourced
from `transformers/csarron/bert-base-uncased-squad-v1`.

members.name: BERTLargeQAWeights
members.extended: The official Google whole-word-masking BERT-Large SQuAD checkpoint.
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import bert_large_qa
>>> model = bert_large_qa(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Devlin, Chang, Lee, Toutanova, *"BERT: Pre-training of
Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding"*, NAACL
2019 ([arXiv:1810.04805](https://arxiv.org/abs/1810.04805)).  Fine-tuned on SQuAD v1.1.

attributes.name: SQUAD_V1
attributes.description:
SQuAD v1.1 fine-tuned checkpoint (F1 93.2 / EM 86.9), sourced
from `transformers/google-bert/bert-large-uncased-whole-word-
masking-finetuned-squad`.

members.name: BERTBaseNERWeights
members.extended:
`dslim/bert-base-NER` — a **cased** BERT-Base (vocab 28 996) with a
9-way BIO tag head (O, B/I-PER, B/I-ORG, B/I-LOC, B/I-MISC).  Tokenize
with the cased `BERTTokenizer` (`do_lower_case=False`).
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import bert_base_token_cls
>>> model = bert_base_token_cls(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Devlin, Chang, Lee, Toutanova, *"BERT: Pre-training of
Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding"*, NAACL
2019 ([arXiv:1810.04805](https://arxiv.org/abs/1810.04805)).  Fine-tuned on CoNLL-2003 NER
(dslim/bert-base-NER).

attributes.name: CONLL2003
attributes.description:
CoNLL-2003 NER fine-tuned checkpoint (F1 91.3), sourced from
`transformers/dslim/bert-base-NER`.

members.name: GPTWeights
members.extended: Radford et al. 2018 12-layer decoder trunk (`H=768, A=12`, 116.5 M params).
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import gpt
>>> model = gpt(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Radford, Narasimhan, Salimans, Sutskever, *"Improving
Language Understanding by Generative Pre-Training"*, OpenAI 2018.

members.name: GPTLMWeights
members.extended: GPT-1 decoder + tied `lm_head` over the 40 478-token BPE vocab.
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import gpt_lm
>>> model = gpt_lm(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Radford, Narasimhan, Salimans, Sutskever, *"Improving
Language Understanding by Generative Pre-Training"*, OpenAI 2018.

attributes.name: BOOKCORPUS
attributes.description:
BookCorpus pre-training checkpoint (decoder + LM head) sourced
from `transformers/openai-community/openai-gpt`.

members.name: RoFormerWeights
members.extended: Su et al. 2021 12-layer rotary encoder trunk (`H=768`, 123.6 M params).
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import roformer
>>> model = roformer(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Su, Lu, Pan, Murtadha, Wen, Liu, *"RoFormer: Enhanced
Transformer with Rotary Position Embedding"*, 2021 ([arXiv:2104.09864](https://arxiv.org/abs/2104.09864)).

attributes.name: CLUECORPUSSMALL
attributes.description:
CLUECorpusSmall Chinese pre-training checkpoint sourced from
`transformers/junnyu/roformer_chinese_base`.

members.name: RoFormerMLMWeights
members.extended: RoFormer encoder + tied MLM head over the 50 000-token Chinese vocab.
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import roformer_mlm
>>> model = roformer_mlm(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Su, Lu, Pan, Murtadha, Wen, Liu, *"RoFormer: Enhanced
Transformer with Rotary Position Embedding"*, 2021 ([arXiv:2104.09864](https://arxiv.org/abs/2104.09864)).

attributes.name: CLUECORPUSSMALL
attributes.description:
CLUECorpusSmall Chinese pre-training checkpoint (encoder + MLM
head) sourced from `transformers/junnyu/roformer_chinese_base`.

extended:
Discovery namespace for every model family's `<Variant>Weights` enum.
Each enum is *declared* inside its model package
(`lucid/models/vision/<family>/_weights.py`) so the declaration sits
right next to the architecture it targets; this module then re-exports
all of them under a single short import path so users don't have to
remember which sub-package each family lives in:

from lucid.models.weights import AlexNetWeights, ResNet18Weights
    from lucid.models import alexnet_cls
    m = alexnet_cls(weights=AlexNetWeights.IMAGENET1K_V1)

Deliberately separate from `lucid.weights` — that package hosts the
*infrastructure* (`WeightEntry`, `WeightsEnum`, `HUB_BASE`,
`register_weights`, hub download, loading); this package only re-exports
*concrete* enums.  And deliberately separate from the top-level
`lucid.models` namespace — that namespace already carries every
model class + factory, so piling on tens of additional `*Weights`
symbols would bury discovery.  All `<Variant>Weights` enums are
reachable here and *only* here:

* ✅ `from lucid.models.weights import AlexNetWeights`
* ❌ `from lucid.models import AlexNetWeights` (not exported)

The dependency direction is one-way (`models.weights` → per-family
`_weights.py` → `lucid.weights`); the per-family declarations
register themselves with `lucid.weights` for the discovery
registry on import, so importing `lucid.models.weights` (or any
member of it) also populates `lucid.weights.list_pretrained`.
